---
title: "KNNandKmeans"
output: html_document
---

```{r}

tele <- read.csv("tele.csv", stringsAsFactors = TRUE)

tele$X <- NULL
tele$duration <- NULL

tele$telepcalled <-ifelse(tele$pdays == 999, 0,1)

str(tele)
summary(tele)

# Convert categorical variables to numeric indicators
# model.matrix creates 'dummy' columns for every factor level
tele_mm <- as.data.frame(model.matrix(~ . - 1, data = tele))
```

```{r}
normalize <- function(x) {
  return ((x - min(x)) / (max(x) - min(x)))
}

# Apply normalization to the dummy-coded data
tele_norm <- as.data.frame(lapply(tele_mm, normalize))

# Assuming 'yyes' is your target after dummy coding
target <- tele_mm$yyes 

# Remove the target from the features used for clustering/training
tele_features <- tele_norm[, !names(tele_norm) %in% c("yyes", "yno")]
```

```{r}

library(class)
library(caret)

set.seed(12345)
ratio <- 0.7

# 1. Create the row indices
train_rows <- sample(1:nrow(tele_norm), ratio*nrow(tele_norm))

# 2. Split the features (tele_norm contains all features except the target)
# We exclude 'yyes' and 'yno' here as you did in your 'tele_features' step
tele_train <- tele_norm[train_rows, !names(tele_norm) %in% c("yyes", "yno")]
tele_test  <- tele_norm[-train_rows, !names(tele_norm) %in% c("yyes", "yno")]

# 3. Split the labels (target)
train_labels <- target[train_rows]
test_labels  <- target[-train_rows]

# 4. Generate Clusters using K-means
# We use the full feature set to define the "tribes"
set.seed(12345)
tele_clusters <- kmeans(tele_norm[, !names(tele_norm) %in% c("yyes", "yno")], centers = 5)

# 5. Add cluster IDs back to your train/test sets so the "Golden Cluster" logic works
tele_train$cluster <- tele_clusters$cluster[train_rows]
tele_test$cluster  <- tele_clusters$cluster[-train_rows]

# --- RENAME FOR YOUR EXISTING CODE ---
# Your snippet uses 'train_data' and 'test_data', so let's point those to our new sets
train_data <- tele_train
test_data  <- tele_test



# --- STEP 1: IDENTIFY THE GOLDEN CLUSTER ---
# Calculate the success rate for each cluster using the training labels
# This finds which "tribe" actually says 'yes' most often
cluster_performance <- tapply(train_labels, train_data$cluster, mean)
golden_cluster_id <- as.numeric(names(which.max(cluster_performance)))

cat("The Golden Cluster is Cluster #", golden_cluster_id, 
    " with a training success rate of ", round(max(cluster_performance)*100, 2), "%\n")

# --- STEP 2: FILTER TEST DATA TO ONLY INCLUDE THE GOLDEN CLUSTER ---
# We only want to 'call' people who fall into this high-probability group
is_golden_test <- test_data$cluster == golden_cluster_id

# Create a subset of the test data and labels for just this cluster
test_data_filtered   <- test_data[is_golden_test, ]
test_labels_filtered <- test_labels[is_golden_test]

# --- STEP 3: RUN KNN ON THE FILTERED DATA ---
# We still use the full train_data/train_labels so the model has a broad 
# understanding of patterns, but we only generate predictions for our target cluster.
predictions_filtered <- knn(train = train_data, 
                            test  = test_data_filtered, 
                            cl    = train_labels, 
                            k     = 18)

# --- STEP 4: EVALUATE & PROFIT CALCULATIONS ---
# Confusion Matrix for the filtered group
cm <- confusionMatrix(as.factor(predictions_filtered), 
                      as.factor(test_labels_filtered), 
                      positive = "1")

# True Positives and False Positives from our selective calling strategy
TP <- cm$table[2,2] 
FP <- cm$table[2,1] 

# Calculate the baseline for the WHOLE test set (to see how much we improved)
total_yes_baseline <- sum(test_labels == 1)
total_calls_baseline <- nrow(test_data)
baseline_success_rate <- total_yes_baseline / total_calls_baseline

# Baseline Profit (Calling everyone in the test set)
baseline_profit <- (10 * total_yes_baseline) - (2 * total_calls_baseline)

# Model Success Rate (Precision within the Golden Cluster)
newsuccessrate <- TP / (TP + FP)

# Your specific efficiency-based cost logic
diff <- newsuccessrate - baseline_success_rate
newrrate <- diff * 10000 

pricepercall <- (1000 / (newrrate + 1000)) + 1

# FINAL PROFIT
# Notice (TP + FP) is much smaller now because we ignored 4 out of 5 clusters!
profit <- (10 * TP) - (pricepercall * (TP + FP))

# Results Output
cat("New Success Rate (Precision):", round(newsuccessrate * 100, 2), "%\n")
cat("Efficiency Adjusted Price Per Call: $", round(pricepercall, 2), "\n")
cat("Total Profit from Cluster-Filtered KNN: $", round(profit, 2), "\n")

```
